𝗖𝗔𝗧𝗩𝗧𝗢𝗡 | Virtual clothing try-on for fashion  🌟 Rising Fast!

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Virtual clothing try-on for fashion
📅 Created: Jul 2024 • Updated: May 2026

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Lightweight Network (899.06M parameters)
✅ Parameter-Efficient Training (49.57M parameters trainable)
✅ Simplified Inference (< 8G VRAM for 1024x768)
✅ Diffusion Model for Virtual Try-On
✅ Mask-free version available
✅ CatVTON-FLUX (LoRA for FLUX.1-Fill-dev)


▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 218 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 67 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

CatVTON is an efficient diffusion model for virtual clothing try-on. It features a lightweight network architecture, parameter-efficient training, and simplified inference, allowing high-resolution try-ons with minimal VRAM.

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Zheng-Chong/CatVTON

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗜𝗖𝗞𝗥𝗦 | Displaying real-time stock market data and charts in a terminal environment.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Displaying real-time stock market data and charts in a terminal environment.
📅 Created: Feb 2020 • Updated: May 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✅ Real-time ticker data display
✅ Terminal User Interface (TUI)
✅ Data sourced from Yahoo! Finance
✅ Customizable chart types (line, candle, kagi)
✅ Configurable time frames and update intervals
✅ Support for pre/post market hours
✅ Multiple installation methods (binary, cargo, package managers)

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Tickrs is a Rust-based command-line application that provides real-time stock ticker data directly in the terminal. It leverages Yahoo! Finance for data and offers various customization options for displaying stock information, charts, and market hours.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗦𝗘𝗘𝗞𝗘𝗥 | To demonstrate and educate users about how malicious websites can exploit location permissions and gather sensitive device information through social engineering, acting as a proof-of-concept for security awareness.  ✨ Notable!

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 CSS 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To demonstrate and educate users about how malicious websites can exploit location permissions and gather sensitive device information through social engineering, acting as a proof-of-concept for security awareness.
📅 Created: Jul 2018 • Updated: May 2026

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Hosts fake websites to request location permissions
✅ Gathers Longitude, Latitude, Accuracy, Altitude, Direction, and Speed
✅ Collects Device Information via Canvas Fingerprinting (Unique ID, Model, OS, CPU Cores, RAM, Screen Resolution, GPU, Browser, Public/Local IP)
✅ Performs Automatic IP Address Reconnaissance
✅ Provides higher accuracy than traditional IP Geolocation by using GPS hardware
✅ Includes various pre-made templates (NearYou, Google Drive, WhatsApp, etc.)


▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 9.5K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2.2K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 10 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Seeker is a tool designed to accurately locate smartphones by hosting a fake website that requests location permissions. Upon user consent, it gathers precise location data (longitude, latitude, accuracy, altitude) and device information (unique ID, model, OS, hardware specs, IP addresses) through social engineering tactics. It serves as a proof-of-concept to educate users about potential data privacy risks from malicious websites.

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/thewhiteh4t/seeker

𝗡𝗘𝗢𝗦𝗛𝗘𝗟𝗟 | Remotely managing and controlling a Windows PC from a smartphone or other web-enabled device over a local Wi-Fi network.  

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Remotely managing and controlling a Windows PC from a smartphone or other web-enabled device over a local Wi-Fi network.
📅 Created: May 2026 • Updated: May 2026

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Remote PC control via Wi-Fi
✅ Web-based interface
✅ PC locking, sleep, shutdown, reboot
✅ Minimize all windows
✅ Task Manager access
✅ Close active application (Alt+F4)
✅ Open links/searches in browser
✅ Launch custom applications
✅ Real-time PC status (PING)
✅ Security with a secret key
✅ Progressive Web App (PWA) support for mobile
✅ Tray icon with status indication
✅ Autostart with Windows


◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 14 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 0 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

NeoShell is a Python-based application that allows users to remotely control their Windows PC from a smartphone via Wi-Fi. It provides a web-based interface accessible from any device on the same network, offering features like PC locking, sleep, shutdown, task management, and application launching.

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Visualizing and understanding the history and evolution of source code repositories in an engaging and interactive 3D environment.
📅 Created: Sep 2009 • Updated: May 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✅ Tree-based display of directories and files
✅ Contributor avatars appearing/disappearing
✅ Interactive playback controls (start/stop dates, speed)
✅ Customizable appearance (colors, background, logo)

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Gource is a C++ visualization tool that renders source control repository history as an interactive 3D tree. It visually represents directories as branches and files as leaves, with contributors appearing and disappearing as they make changes. The tool allows for real-time playback and customization of the visualization.
